model.model_name: Elo and team-quality adjusted Poisson score model
model.plain_language_summary: Estimate each team's expected goals from attack proxy, opponent defense proxy, team-quality gap, Elo gap, and host-venue boost. Convert expected goals into scoreline probabilities with a Poisson score grid.

future_upgrade_triggers.v1: Upgrade after final squads, official fantasy prices, official scoring rules, updated team ratings, injuries, and pre-tournament national-team form are imported.
future_upgrade_triggers.v2: Upgrade after v1 is stable and we are ready to calibrate low-score/draw behavior with historical matches or early tournament results using Dixon-Coles-style correction.

model_notes: This is the first score-prediction model for fantasy planning, not a betting model.
It is intentionally transparent and uses no betting odds.
Use expected goals and clean-sheet probabilities as match environment signals for player recommendations.
Replace or recalibrate after final rosters, official prices, and official fantasy scoring are available.
Step 6 hardening added fixture coverage, probability bounds, xG guardrails, favorite consistency, top-scoreline, input coverage, and player-matchday integration checks.
PELE ratings are still null by design and are not imputed.

fixtureScorePredictions.source_note: Prototype score prediction from data/fixtures.json and data/teamQuality.json. No betting odds, final squads, injuries, official fantasy prices, or official fantasy scoring are used.

caveats: Expected goals are model estimates, not source-provided bookmaker odds or official projections.
The model does not yet include final squads, player availability, injuries, tactics, recent friendlies, or official fantasy scoring.
Poisson assumes independent team goal counts; Dixon-Coles or similar low-score correction is reserved for a later version.
